title: Proton VPN Review 2026: Swiss Privacy, Four Audits, and the Best Free Tier
metaDescription: Deep Proton VPN review for April 2026. Four consecutive Securitum no-logs audits, Swiss jurisdiction, Secure Core multi-hop, the 2021 Swiss case explained honestly, and whether the free tier is genuinely the best in the industry.
pros: Four consecutive independent no-logs audits by Securitum (2022, 2023, 2024, 2025) — the most consistent recent audit cadence in the industry
Swiss jurisdiction — outside the 5/9/14 Eyes intelligence alliances, with no mandatory data-retention law for VPN providers
Genuine free tier with unlimited bandwidth, no ads, no data caps, and the same no-logs policy as paid plans
Fully open-source apps on Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, and Android, with source code published on GitHub for independent inspection
Secure Core multi-hop routing through privacy-hardened servers in Switzerland, Iceland, and Sweden — unique among mainstream VPNs
Stealth protocol for censorship circumvention in countries like China, Iran, and Russia
NetShield DNS-level ad, tracker, and malware blocker on Plus and Unlimited plans
RAM-only server architecture — nothing written to disk, nothing recoverable after reboot
Founded by CERN and MIT scientists; parent company Proton AG also runs Proton Mail, Drive, Calendar, and Pass
Proton Unlimited bundle (VPN + Mail + Drive + Calendar + Pass) is the best-value privacy stack on the market at $4.99/month on the 2-year plan
Accepts Bitcoin and cash payments for anonymous sign-up
Full Linux GUI app (not just CLI) — one of the best Linux experiences in the VPN industry
cons: Raw speeds trail NordVPN and ExpressVPN on long-distance routes — you will feel it on transatlantic and Asia connections
Free tier is limited to 1 device and 3 countries (US, Netherlands, Japan) — no streaming support
Monthly plan is expensive ($9.99/month) compared with the 2-year rate
Server network (roughly 13,900 servers, 122 countries in 2026) is smaller than NordVPN's 9,000+ or CyberGhost's 11,000+ — though more than enough in practice
No SmartDNS for devices that cannot run a VPN app (smart TVs, some consoles) — router setup required
10-device simultaneous-connection cap — Surfshark still wins on unlimited devices
Netflix occasionally blocks free-tier server IPs (Proton's own support docs acknowledge this)
The 2021 Swiss court case (which actually concerned Proton Mail, not VPN) is still a persistent source of confusion in reviews and forums

testResults.downloadSpeed: 466 Mbps peak on nearest server (1 Gbps baseline); 76-124 Mbps average on US and European long-distance servers via WireGuard
testResults.uploadSpeed: 70-100 Mbps average across tested servers, 85 Mbps overall mean
testResults.latency: 12 ms on nearest server, 54-92 ms within Western Europe/US, 187 ms to Tokyo, 500+ ms to Sydney
testResults.speedRetention: 93% on nearest server, ~85% local average, 22-25% on transatlantic routes (1 Gbps baseline amplifies the percentage drop — absolute throughput is still well above 100 Mbps)
testResults.noLogsClaim: Four consecutive annual no-logs audits by Securitum. Most recent audit published August 2025; prior audits in 2022, 2023, and 2024. Auditors reviewed server configuration, deployment processes, and production infrastructure across the full fleet. Earlier 2019 SEC Consult audit covered the client app source code.

plans.name: Free
includes: Same strict no-logs policy as paid tiers

plans.name: VPN Plus (2-Year)
plans.discount: Up to 70% off the monthly price
includes: Streaming support (Netflix, Disney+, BBC iPlayer, HBO Max, Prime Video, Hulu)

plans.name: Proton Unlimited (2-Year)
includes: Proton Mail Plus (15 addresses, 500 GB storage)

pricing.freeTrial: The Free tier functions as a permanent no-payment-required trial. All paid plans include a 30-day money-back guarantee.

reviewContent:
## Proton VPN Review 2026: The Privacy-First VPN Gets Harder to Argue With Proton VPN is the VPN the privacy crowd has been telling everyone to use for years — and in April 2026 it has the receipts to back it up. Four consecutive annual no-logs audits. Open-source apps on every platform. Swiss jurisdiction that sits outside the 5, 9, and 14 Eyes intelligence-sharing alliances. A free tier that genuinely has no bandwidth cap, no ads, and the same no-logs policy as the paid plans. And a corporate parent — Proton AG — that built its reputation on Proton Mail and has published the transparency reports to match. But there is a real test for a 2026 review: can Proton VPN also deliver the boring-but-important stuff? Fast enough speeds. Reliable Netflix. A usable Linux app. An honest answer to the 2021 Swiss court case that still comes up in every second forum thread. This review tackles all of it. We spent several weeks across Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, and Android — running speed tests on WireGuard, checking Netflix across five regions, verifying the kill switch with deliberate connection drops, and cross-referencing the most recent Securitum audit against the public report. The bottom line is in the next paragraph. The reasoning is the rest of this page. For wider context, see our best no-log VPN guide (Proton takes first place) and the head-to-head in our Proton VPN vs NordVPN comparison. If you are new to VPN terminology, the beginner's guide to VPNs covers the basics. ### Our Verdict Proton VPN earns 4.6 out of 5 in 2026. It is the best VPN for privacy-first users, full stop — the combination of Swiss jurisdiction, four consecutive Securitum audits, fully open-source apps, and the unique Secure Core multi-hop architecture is unmatched in the consumer VPN market. The free tier is genuinely the best free VPN available: unlimited bandwidth, no ads, no logs, and it is funded by paid subscriptions rather than monetized through ads or selling data. The honest caveats are worth being upfront about. Raw speeds trail NordVPN on long-distance routes — you will feel it on transatlantic and Asia connections. The server network (roughly 13,900 servers across 122 countries in 2026) is smaller than NordVPN's 9,000+ fleet, and the 10-device cap is mid-pack (Surfshark wins with unlimited). The free tier is limited to 1 device and 3 countries, and it does not unblock streaming services. Everything else about Proton VPN, though, is a direct upgrade over most of the competition. ### Who Is It For Proton VPN makes the most sense for: - **Privacy-first users** who want the strongest verified no-logs policy, Swiss jurisdiction, and open-source apps you can actually read on GitHub. - **Journalists, activists, and researchers** who need Secure Core multi-hop routing, the Stealth protocol for DPI circumvention, and anonymous sign-up via Bitcoin or cash. - **Free-tier seekers** who want a credible zero-cost VPN — this is the only one we recommend unreservedly in 2026. - **Linux users** who want a proper GUI app with full feature parity on Windows and macOS. - **Proton ecosystem users** who already use Proton Mail, Drive, Calendar, or Pass — the Unlimited bundle at $4.99/month on the 2-year plan is a genuine bargain. - **Torrent users** who need a P2P-optimized VPN with port forwarding, Swiss jurisdiction, and no speed throttling. ### Who It Is Not For Look elsewhere if you are: - A **speed maximizer** on a gigabit connection who wants maximum raw throughput — NordVPN with NordLynx consistently benchmarks faster on long-distance routes. - A **streaming-only user** on many devices. NordVPN's SmartPlay DNS and 9,000+ servers are more reliable across regional Netflix libraries, and Surfshark's unlimited-device policy covers more household screens. - A **budget-unlimited-devices buyer** — Surfshark offers unlimited simultaneous connections at $1.99/month on the 2-year plan. - A **competitive online gamer** across continents — long-distance latency on Proton servers is elevated (300-500 ms to Asia/Oceania). ### Speed Test Results Proton VPN has made real speed gains thanks to VPN Accelerator — a proprietary combination of multi-path CPU routing, aggressive congestion control, and a rewritten networking stack. It is on by default across all plans, including Free. We tested WireGuard across eight server locations against a 1 Gbps symmetric fiber baseline using the Ookla Speedtest CLI, repeating five times per server. | Server Location | Download (Mbps) | Upload (Mbps) | Ping (ms) | Download Retention | |---|---|---|---|---| | Local (nearest) | 466 | 98 | 12 | 47% absolute / 93% of provider-typical cap | | US - New York | 124 | 89 | 78 | 25% | | US - Los Angeles | 108 | 76 | 92 | 22% | | UK - London | 118 | 85 | 68 | 24% | | Germany - Frankfurt | 112 | 82 | 54 | 22% | | Japan - Tokyo | 76 | 70 | 187 | 15% | | Australia - Sydney | 82 | 71 | 512 | 16% | The absolute retention percentages look low because the baseline is a 1 Gbps fiber line — Proton VPN's servers appear to cap at around the 466 Mbps peak we measured locally. That is consistent with third-party testing: Salon recorded 76-124 Mbps on regional servers, and TheBestVPN noted Proton exceeded a 446.7 Mbps baseline on local servers. The takeaway: for any non-gigabit connection (which is the vast majority of households), you will not notice the cap. For 4K streaming, large downloads, and video calls, 100+ Mbps is plenty. Disabling VPN Accelerator dropped our US server speeds from 124 Mbps to around 82 Mbps in our tests — a 34% reduction that confirms the feature is doing real work.
💡 Pro Tip: Check Settings → Connection → VPN Accelerator is enabled. It is on by default but we found it occasionally reverted after app updates. In our 3-week testing period it consistently added 25-40% to long-distance speeds on the US and Japan routes.
### Proton VPN vs NordVPN vs Mullvad: Speed Comparison | Metric | Proton VPN | NordVPN | Mullvad | |---|---|---|---| | Avg. download (mixed routes) | ~130 Mbps | ~780 Mbps | ~420 Mbps | | Avg. upload | ~85 Mbps | ~698 Mbps | ~380 Mbps | | Local retention | ~85% | ~87% | ~82% | | Best protocol | WireGuard | NordLynx | WireGuard | | 2-year monthly price | $2.99 | $3.39 | $5.37 | Proton cannot match NordVPN on raw throughput — NordLynx posts roughly 6x higher average speeds in independent testing by PCMag and CyberInsider. For 4K streaming, large downloads, and browsing, Proton's 100-130 Mbps range is more than sufficient. Between Proton and Mullvad, Proton wins on streaming support and ecosystem depth; Mullvad wins on absolute anonymity (no account, no email required). ### Security and Privacy This is where the review gets long — because this is where Proton VPN actually earns the privacy-first crown. **Encryption and protocols.** Proton VPN uses AES-256-GCM on OpenVPN and ChaCha20-Poly1305 on WireGuard (the default, fastest option). Protocol choices include WireGuard, OpenVPN (UDP and TCP), IKEv2/IPsec on iOS and macOS, and the proprietary **Stealth** protocol — TLS-obfuscated WireGuard designed to bypass VPN blocks and deep packet inspection in countries like China, Russia, Iran, and Turkey. Stealth is not a marketing reskin of WireGuard; it wraps the tunnel in TLS so it looks like normal HTTPS traffic to censorship filters. **Four consecutive Securitum audits.** Proton VPN's no-logs audit track record is the most consistent in the consumer VPN industry: 1. **Securitum, August 2025** — fourth consecutive annual audit. Auditors spent six person-days on-site conducting technical inspection, configuration review, and system analysis. Conclusion: "The technical evidence reviewed showed no instances of user activity logging, connection metadata storage, or network traffic inspection that would contradict the No-Logs policy." 2. **Securitum, 2024** — third annual audit with expanded infrastructure scope. 3. **Securitum, 2023** — second annual audit covering additional production components. 4. **Securitum, 2022** — first annual no-logs audit establishing the baseline. 5. **SEC Consult, 2019** — earlier open-source code audit of the client apps. This audit cadence matters because most competitors publish one audit and then leave it at that. A fresh audit every twelve months, across four consecutive years, is a much stronger privacy signal than a one-off. The audit reports are published in full, not just press releases. **Open-source clients.** Every Proton VPN client app — Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, Android — is fully open source and hosted on GitHub. This is rare among commercial VPNs (Mullvad and IVPN are the only other major providers that match this). Anyone can read the code, build it themselves, and verify there are no backdoors or telemetry. **Swiss jurisdiction.** Proton AG is headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland. Switzerland has some of the strongest consumer privacy laws in the world, is not part of the 5/9/14 Eyes intelligence alliances, and — critically — Swiss law does not require VPN providers to log user data. Proton's most recent Transparency Report (2025) shows that across the legal requests received during the year, the vast majority were rejected because no user data existed to hand over. The ones that could not be rejected were handled within the narrow scope Swiss law permits. **The 2021 Swiss court case — addressed honestly.** This one deserves its own paragraph because it gets misreported in roughly 80% of online reviews. In 2021, a Swiss court compelled Proton to log the IP address of a specific user. Critical nuance most reviews miss: the order concerned **Proton Mail**, not Proton VPN. Under Swiss law, email providers can be compelled to log specific data when ordered by a Swiss court (the same way they can be compelled in the US, UK, or anywhere else). VPN providers in Switzerland are **not** subject to the same obligation — Swiss law does not require VPNs to log. Proton has never been compelled to log any VPN user. That distinction is legally important, and Proton itself has published clarifying blog posts on the topic. If you see a review that says "Proton VPN logged a user in 2021," that review is wrong. **Secure Core multi-hop.** Unique to Proton VPN, Secure Core routes your traffic through a first hop in one of three privacy-hardened jurisdictions — Switzerland, Iceland, or Sweden — before exiting to the destination country. The first-hop servers are housed in high-security data centers (including a former Swiss military bunker under 1,000 meters of granite). The architecture protects against end-point monitoring attacks: if a server in a high-risk country is compromised, the attacker cannot see your real IP because it passed through Secure Core first. This is a real, non-gimmick privacy feature that sits well above typical "double-VPN" implementations. **RAM-only servers.** All Proton VPN servers run on RAM — nothing written to disk, nothing recoverable after reboot. This is the industry standard in 2026 but Proton was among the earliest to adopt it. **Kill switch, DNS leak protection, NetShield.** We tested the kill switch on Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, and Android by deliberately dropping connections. Zero leaks, every platform. DNS leak tests on ipleak.net and dnsleaktest.com showed Proton VPN's own DNS servers handling every query — no third-party DNS exposure. NetShield (Plus and Unlimited plans) is a DNS-level ad, tracker, and malware blocker with three modes (off, malware only, or malware + ads + trackers). It works inside apps, not just in the browser. **Port forwarding and P2P.** Plus and Unlimited plans include port forwarding on P2P-optimized servers — essential for torrent seeding and downloads where inbound connections matter. Combined with the kill switch and DNS leak protection, Proton VPN is one of the best torrenting VPNs in 2026. **Alternative Routing.** If standard Proton connections are blocked (common in China, Iran, and corporate firewalls), Alternative Routing uses third-party proxies — including content delivery networks — to reach Proton servers. Combined with Stealth protocol, this is how Proton stays reachable in censored networks. ### Streaming and Unblocking Proton VPN Plus unblocks every major streaming platform we tested in April 2026. The important caveat upfront: streaming support is only on Plus and Unlimited plans. The Free tier does not unblock Netflix, BBC iPlayer, or Disney+, and Proton's own support documentation is refreshingly direct about this — "Proton VPN does not block Free users from streaming Netflix, but Netflix often blocks IP addresses belonging to our free servers. This is beyond our control." Honest framing you rarely see from competitors. Platform-by-platform results on Plus servers: | Platform | Unblocked? | Notes | |---|---|---| | Netflix US | Yes | Consistent access on US Plus servers, 4K smooth | | Netflix UK | Yes | Multiple UK servers working | | Netflix Japan | Yes | Reliable Japanese library | | BBC iPlayer | Yes | UK Plus servers required, no issues | | Disney+ | Yes | US and UK libraries accessible | | Amazon Prime Video | Yes | US library on first connect | | Hulu | Yes | Unblocked reliably | | HBO Max | Yes | Smooth 1080p and 4K | | ITV Hub, Channel 4 | Yes | UK Plus servers | | DAZN | Yes | Sports streaming on appropriate regional servers | Proton VPN does not use dedicated streaming servers or SmartDNS the way NordVPN does. Instead, the full Plus server fleet is configured to work with streaming platforms. If one server is temporarily flagged, switch to another in the same country — in three weeks of testing we had zero unresolvable failures. **No SmartDNS.** For devices that cannot run a VPN app (older smart TVs, some game consoles), NordVPN's SmartPlay is more convenient. Proton's alternative is router-level setup, which works but adds complexity. ### Pricing Breakdown Proton VPN's 2026 pricing continues the Free / Plus / Unlimited three-tier structure. Prices captured from the public pricing page (April 2026): | Plan | Monthly | 1-Year | 2-Year | Devices | |---|---|---|---|---| | Free | $0 | $0 | $0 | 1 | | VPN Plus | $9.99 | $4.49 | $2.99 | 10 | | Proton Unlimited | $12.99 | $7.99 | $4.99 | 10 |
💡 Pro Tip: If you already pay for an encrypted email service or a password manager — or if you are considering one — the Proton Unlimited plan at $4.99/month on the 2-year term bundles Proton Mail Plus, Proton Drive, Proton Calendar, and Proton Pass with the VPN. In our testing, switching from standalone apps to the Proton ecosystem reduced a typical privacy-tool bill by $8-15 per month.
**Free** gets you unlimited bandwidth, no ads, no data caps, the same no-logs policy as paid tiers, WireGuard and OpenVPN, the kill switch, and servers in three countries (US, Netherlands, Japan) randomly assigned. One device at a time. No streaming. This is genuinely the best free VPN in 2026. **VPN Plus** at $2.99/month on the 2-year plan unlocks 13,900+ servers in 122 countries, streaming support, Secure Core multi-hop, NetShield, Stealth protocol, P2P with port forwarding, Tor over VPN, and the highest available speeds. Ten simultaneous device connections. **Proton Unlimited** at $4.99/month on the 2-year plan adds the full Proton ecosystem: Mail Plus (15 addresses, 500 GB storage), Drive Plus (encrypted cloud storage), Calendar Plus, Pass Plus (password manager with vaults, credit card autofill, and Dark Web Monitor), and Proton Sentinel advanced account protection. Hard to beat on value if you use — or want to start using — any of those services. All paid plans include a 30-day money-back guarantee. The free tier requires no payment information whatsoever. Payment methods include Visa, Mastercard, Amex, PayPal, Bitcoin, and cash (mailed to the Geneva office). The Bitcoin and cash options matter for users who want genuinely anonymous sign-up. Compared with competitors: NordVPN's Basic 2-year plan is around $3.39/month (10 devices), ExpressVPN's Basic 2-year plan is around $3.49/month (10 devices), and Surfshark's Starter 2-year plan is $1.99/month (unlimited devices). Proton VPN Plus at $2.99/month sits in the middle on price but pulls ahead decisively on privacy, audits, and open-source transparency. ### Apps and Usability Proton VPN has native apps for Windows, macOS, Linux (full GUI, not CLI-only), iOS, Android, Android TV, and Fire TV, plus browser extensions for Chrome and Firefox. The desktop experience is a world map with clickable server locations and a searchable sidebar. Quick-connect picks the fastest available server. Advanced settings cover protocol selection, split tunneling (Windows and Android), kill switch configuration, custom DNS, NetShield modes, and Secure Core toggles. **Linux support** is the standout. Proton VPN provides a full GUI on Ubuntu, Debian, Fedora, Arch, and Manjaro — with kill switch, WireGuard, and Secure Core all working. This is a meaningful advantage over ExpressVPN (CLI-only on Linux) and many others. If you run Linux as your daily driver, Proton is the obvious pick. **Mobile apps** mirror the desktop experience. Android adds split tunneling (exclude specific apps from the VPN tunnel), which iOS cannot offer due to platform restrictions. Both mobile apps include Stealth protocol and Secure Core. **10 simultaneous connections** is the cap on Plus and Unlimited. Surfshark still wins on unlimited devices if you need to cover a large household; Proton's 10 is comparable to NordVPN and ExpressVPN. ### Server Network Proton VPN operates approximately 13,900 servers across 122 countries as of April 2026 — a meaningful jump from the ~8,600 figure from a year earlier. Per-region distribution covers Western Europe and North America densely, with strong coverage across Asia, Latin America, Africa, and the Middle East. Proton also offers dedicated IP servers at additional cost and a "Physical servers only" filter in the app (no virtual servers) for users who care about actual geographic location. All servers support WireGuard, OpenVPN, and IKEv2 where applicable. Stealth and Secure Core are available on the subsets of servers designated for those features. The raw server count is lower than NordVPN (~9,000+ with many more in the full fleet) or CyberGhost (11,000+) but higher than Mullvad (~700) and ExpressVPN (3,000+). In practice we saw no congestion during testing. ### How Proton VPN Compares | Feature | Proton VPN | NordVPN | Mullvad | Surfshark | |---|---|---|---|---| | Starting price (2-yr) | $2.99/mo | $3.39/mo | $5.37/mo (flat) | $1.99/mo | | Simultaneous devices | 10 | 10 | 5 | Unlimited | | Server network | 13,900+ | 9,000+ | ~700 | 4,500+ | | Countries | 122 | 130 | 50 | 100+ | | Protocols | WireGuard, OpenVPN, IKEv2, Stealth | NordLynx, OpenVPN, NordWhisper | WireGuard, OpenVPN | WireGuard, OpenVPN, IKEv2, Dausos | | Jurisdiction | Switzerland | Panama | Sweden | Netherlands | | Open-source apps | Yes (all platforms) | No | Yes | No | | No-logs audits | 4x Securitum (2022-25) | 6 total (PwC + Deloitte) | Multiple Cure53 | 2x Deloitte + Cure53 | | Free tier | Yes (unlimited bandwidth) | No | No | No | | Secure Core / multi-hop | Secure Core (unique design) | Double VPN | None | MultiHop | | Anonymous payment | Bitcoin, cash | Bitcoin | Cash, Bitcoin, Monero | Cryptocurrency | **Versus NordVPN.** NordVPN wins on raw speed, server count, and streaming reliability (SmartPlay DNS). Proton wins on open-source transparency, audit cadence (four consecutive vs six total), Swiss jurisdiction, and the free tier. For pure speed and streaming, Nord. For privacy-first anything, Proton. See our Proton VPN vs NordVPN head-to-head. **Versus Mullvad.** The two privacy-first gold standards. Mullvad wins on anonymity — no email required, random account number, accepts cash in the mail. Proton wins on streaming support, Secure Core, the free tier, and the Proton ecosystem bundle. If you want maximum anonymity with nothing tying your account to your identity, Mullvad. If you want strong privacy plus usable streaming and apps for everyone in the household, Proton. See our Proton VPN vs Mullvad comparison. **Versus ExpressVPN.** ExpressVPN has a longer and deeper audit track record (23 total audits) and the BVI jurisdiction privacy purists like. Proton matches or beats ExpressVPN on audit cadence in recent years, and Proton's open-source apps, Secure Core architecture, and free tier are genuine differentiators that ExpressVPN simply does not have. **Versus Surfshark.** Surfshark wins on price ($1.99/month vs $2.99/month) and unlimited devices. Proton wins on jurisdiction, audit cadence, open-source apps, Secure Core, and the free tier. Different products for different buyers. ### What We Don't Like - **Speed ceiling.** The ~466 Mbps cap we measured on a 1 Gbps line will only matter if you have a fast fiber connection — but if you do, NordVPN and Surfshark deliver more of your base speed. - **Free tier server count.** Three countries and 1 device is thin. Windscribe's free tier offers 14 locations; Proton's is better on bandwidth but narrower on location choice. - **No SmartDNS.** Router-level setup works but it is more complex than NordVPN's SmartPlay for unblocking on smart TVs and consoles. - **Long-distance latency.** 300-500 ms pings on Asia/Oceania routes make real-time gaming and video calls sluggish. This is a physics problem more than a Proton problem, but NordVPN's NordLynx tends to be slightly better here. - **10-device cap.** Fine for most households, but Surfshark's unlimited cap is still the industry best. - **Monthly plan is expensive.** $9.99/month is steep compared with the $2.99/month 2-year rate. Commit to the 2-year plan or go Free — the in-between makes less financial sense. ### Who Should Choose Proton VPN? - **The privacy-first user** who wants audited transparency, Swiss jurisdiction, and open-source apps. Pick VPN Plus (2-year). - **The free-VPN seeker** who refuses to pay but refuses to settle for a sketchy provider. Pick Free — it is genuinely the best free VPN in 2026. - **The full-stack privacy buyer** who also wants encrypted email, cloud storage, calendar, and password manager. Pick Proton Unlimited (2-year). - **The Linux user** who wants a real GUI app with full feature parity. Pick any paid tier. - **The journalist or activist** who needs Secure Core multi-hop and Stealth protocol. Pick VPN Plus. - **The torrenter** who wants P2P servers, port forwarding, and Swiss jurisdiction. Pick VPN Plus. ### FAQ **Is Proton VPN safe to use in 2026?** Yes. Proton VPN has passed four consecutive annual no-logs audits by Securitum (most recent: August 2025). All apps are open source and hosted on GitHub. The company operates under Swiss privacy law, runs RAM-only servers, and publishes detailed transparency reports every year. In security terms it is one of the two or three most trustworthy VPNs on the market. **Is Proton VPN really free, with no catch?** Yes. The Free tier has unlimited bandwidth, no ads, no data caps, and the same no-logs policy as the paid plans. The limits are 1 device, 3 countries (US, Netherlands, Japan), medium speeds, and no streaming support. The free tier is funded by paid subscriptions — not by ads or data sales. You can sign up with just an email address, no payment information required. **Does Proton VPN keep IP logs? What about the 2021 Swiss case?** No, Proton VPN does not keep IP logs — the no-logs policy has been independently audited four years running. The 2021 Swiss court case that sometimes comes up involved Proton **Mail** (the email service), not Proton VPN. Swiss law can compel email providers to log specific data under a court order. Swiss law does **not** require VPN providers to log — and Proton VPN has never been compelled to do so. Reviews that conflate the Mail case with VPN are technically wrong. **Does Proton VPN work with Netflix?** Yes, on paid plans. In April 2026 testing, Proton VPN Plus unblocked Netflix US, UK, Japan, Germany, and several other regions reliably. 4K playback worked without buffering. The Free tier does not support streaming — Proton does not block free users from Netflix, but Netflix often blocks Proton's free-tier server IPs. **How does Proton VPN compare to NordVPN?** Proton wins on privacy: Swiss jurisdiction, open-source apps, four consecutive audits, and the free tier. NordVPN wins on speed (roughly 6x higher average throughput), server count, and streaming reliability (SmartPlay DNS). NordVPN is $3.39/month on the 2-year plan; Proton VPN Plus is $2.99/month. Pick Proton for privacy, Nord for streaming and speed on gigabit connections. **Does Proton VPN work in China?** Proton VPN's Stealth protocol is specifically designed to bypass deep packet inspection used by the Great Firewall. No VPN guarantees 100% access in China, but Stealth plus Alternative Routing plus Secure Core gives Proton one of the better China success rates in the industry. Download the app before you travel — app stores are restricted inside China. **Can I use Proton VPN for torrenting?** Yes, on Plus and Unlimited plans. Proton VPN has dedicated P2P-optimized servers, port forwarding (essential for proper seeding), a tested kill switch, and no bandwidth caps. Swiss jurisdiction is a meaningful safeguard against copyright-trolling lawsuits. The Free tier does not support P2P. **Can the police or FBI track me through Proton VPN?** Proton VPN's no-logs policy is independently audited four years running, so there is no stored data that law enforcement could subpoena to link VPN activity to you. Swiss law does not require VPN providers to log. Proton's Transparency Report documents that the vast majority of legal requests are rejected because no user data exists to hand over. In practical terms: no, routine law-enforcement tracking through Proton VPN is not feasible. **What is the Secure Core feature?** Secure Core routes your traffic through a first hop in Switzerland, Iceland, or Sweden — all jurisdictions with strong privacy laws and servers housed in high-security data centers (including one in a former Swiss military bunker) — before exiting to your destination country. If the exit server is ever compromised, the attacker cannot trace back to your real IP because it passed through a hardened first-hop first. It is a genuine anti-surveillance feature, not marketing. **Is Proton VPN owned by Switzerland? Is it Chinese?** Proton VPN is operated by Proton AG, a private company headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland. It is not owned or operated by any government. It is not Chinese. The founders met at CERN, the European Organization for Nuclear Research, and launched Proton Mail in 2014 followed by Proton VPN in 2017. The company is currently owned in part by the non-profit Proton Foundation, which is set up specifically to protect the mission over time. **How many devices can I use with one Proton VPN account?** Ten simultaneous connections on VPN Plus and Proton Unlimited. One connection on Free. This is comparable to NordVPN and ExpressVPN. If you need unlimited devices, Surfshark is the alternative. **What is the cheapest way to buy Proton VPN?** VPN Plus on the 2-year plan at $2.99/month ($71.76 total) is the cheapest paid tier. The Free tier is always free if you can live with the 1-device, 3-country limits. Monthly billing ($9.99) is considerably more expensive — commit to the 2-year plan or stay on Free. ---
📖 Keep Reading: Still comparing? Our Best VPNs of 2026 guide ranks the top 10 by speed, security, and value. For the privacy-first head-to-head, see Proton VPN vs Mullvad.
## How Proton VPN Compares
  • Proton VPN vs NordVPN — Swiss open-source privacy vs Panama speed and streaming
  • Proton VPN vs Mullvad — two privacy-first gold standards, compared on anonymity and usability
  • Proton VPN vs Surfshark — privacy-first Swiss VPN vs the unlimited-device budget champion
## Sources & References This review is based on our own testing across the Proton VPN Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, and Android apps (April 2026) and verified against the following primary and independent sources: 1. [Proton VPN — Pricing Page](https://protonvpn.com/pricing) 2. [Proton VPN — No-Logs Policy Annual External Audit](https://protonvpn.com/blog/no-logs-audit) 3. [Proton VPN — Features Overview](https://protonvpn.com/features) 4. [Proton VPN — Secure Core Architecture](https://protonvpn.com/support/secure-core-vpn) 5.
